g.text: reach: every namespace in the cluster
a user namespace, per AgentChannel / per AgentTask
Three edge classes, and the two RBAC facts that force them.
Fact 1: a ClusterRole bound with a ClusterRoleBinding applies in every namespace.
There is no way to say
"this grant, but only in agentry-system". So anything that must be namespace-limited ships as a separate
namespaced Role, which is why the gateway has a ClusterRole/Role
rather than one object, and why
is an honest cluster-wide grant: it cannot be narrowed to "user namespaces only". Service
objects carry no secret material, so the extra reach is benign.
Fact 2: resourceNames constrains get, update, patch, delete and watch, but NOT list and NOT create.
dotted grants therefore omit a verb on purpose.
is dead weight on a name-scoped Secret read, and a
must set fieldSelector metadata.name=<secret> to satisfy the check.
load-bearing omission: granting create on a named ConfigMap would silently widen the gateway to
ConfigMaps in that namespace, so the reconciler pre-creates the object and grants only get, update, patch.
delete on agentry-system ConfigMaps is absent for a different reason:
ModelProviderReconciler prunes stale budget keys; the AgentChannelReconciler prunes and finalizer-sweeps
